mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, household income stability improves and consumer spending accelerates, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicate synchronized growth in construction activity and consumer labor income, which jointly amplify aggregate demand and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in more than 60% of rolling 12-month windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index signals capacity constraints and supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 50% of instances where INDPRO declines for three consecutive months, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.5 percent year-over-year growth threshold, markets price in aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 generates positive forward returns in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL crosses the 3.5% threshold, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales figures directly reflect stronger consumer expenditure, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: The forward return distribution for positive RSAFS months does not statistically differ from the overall market distribution (Kolmogorov-Smirnov test p > 0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate and declines in total nonfarm payrolls confirm a deteriorating labor market, which triggers recession fears and negative earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of confirmed labor market deterioration episodes over a 3-year out-of-sample period, or the interaction effect vanishes in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index during early-cycle recoveries indicates rebuilding capacity and rising business investment, which supports corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean during INDPRO expansion phases (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal produces negative returns in over 60% of triggered windows.
